title: ConvertKit API keys should not be disclosed
description: ConvertKit (now Kit) is an email marketing platform for creators. API keys and secrets authenticate requests to manage subscribers, sequences, forms, and broadcasts. Exposing credentials allows unauthorized access to subscriber data and email sending.
recommendation: Remove ConvertKit API keys from source code. Store in environment variables. Rotate at app.convertkit.com/account_settings/advanced_settings.
impact: Unauthorized access to subscriber lists with PII, email broadcast sending, sequence manipulation, and form data exposure.

checks.message: ConvertKit API secret detected. ConvertKit API secrets authenticate full access to your email marketing account including subscriber management and tagging, creating and sending broadcasts, managing sequences and automations, accessing form data and subscriber PII, and webhook configuration. Exposing this secret allows unauthorized control of your email list. Rotate immediately at app.convertkit.com/account_settings/advanced_settings.
ConvertKit API key detected. ConvertKit API keys provide read access to subscriber and form data. Rotate at app.convertkit.com/account_settings/advanced_settings.
